summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a blistering August ’72 Berkeley set, highlighted by a wild Hey Bo Diddley, soaring The Other One and a scorching Black Throated Wind.
review: The Miller‑remastered two‑track off‑the‑board mix delivers crystal‑clear instrument separation with only minor tape glitches, making this one of the most listenable 1972 recordings. Musically the band erupts from a mellow opening into a fireworks‑filled second set – Jerry’s dizzying licks, Keith’s piano vibe, Phil’s thunderous bass and an ultra‑rare slide‑driven Hey Bo Diddley are repeatedly praised. Overall the performance is seen as near‑perfect for the era.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both the musical performance and the soundboard recording across many detailed reviews; minor tape cut noted but does not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Two‑track off‑the‑board mix with superb clarity and instrument separation; a few minor glitches and an abrupt tape cut at the end are the only noted issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Starts mellow, builds to a high‑energy first set climax, second set warms up and delivers a fire‑storm finish with an epic Other One and the rare Hey Bo Diddley sandwich.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as riffing dizzying licks, delivering wild, fluttery solos and a squeaking white Strat that thrilled listeners.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for being on top of his game with a thunderous bass workout and an epic solo during The Other One; vocals noted as strong before later voice changes.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano/vibe is highlighted as locking in perfectly, while Bobby (organ) adds gleeful counter‑punches throughout the set.
